1→"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2→
     3→In CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4→CLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5→if the user is not logged in.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→
    11→from patchright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.panel import Panel
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    19→
    20→console = Console()
    21→
    22→
    23→async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    24→    """
    25→    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    26→
    27→    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    28→    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    29→
    30→    Args:
    31→        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    32→
    33→    Returns:
    34→        True if logged in
    35→    """
    36→    if browser is None:
    37→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    40→    page = await browser.start()
    41→
    42→    # Check if already logged in
    43→    if await check_login_status(page):
    44→        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
    45→        # Save session as backup
    46→        await browser.save_session()
    47→        return True
    48→
    49→    # Not logged in — guide the user
    50→    console.print(Panel(
    51→        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    52→        "Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser:\n"
    53→        "1. Go to https://maimai.cn/login in Chrome\n"
    54→        "2. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    55→        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    56→        "[dim]Then run this command again to verify.[/dim]",
    57→        title="Maimai Login",
    58→        border_style="cyan"
    59→    ))
    60→
    61→    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    62→    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    63→    poll_interval = 3
    64→    elapsed = 0
    65→
    66→    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    67→
    68→    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    69→        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    70→        elapsed += poll_interval
    71→
    72→        # Check for captcha
    73→        await check_captcha(page)
    74→
    75→        if await check_login_status(page):
    76→            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    77→            await browser.save_session()
    78→            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    79→            return True
    80→
    81→    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    82→    return False
    83→
    84→
    85→async def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    86→    """
    87→    Check if user is currently logged in by navigating to a protected page
    88→    and observing whether Maimai redirects to the login page.
    89→
    90→    API probes can give false positives (server returns 200 but session is
    91→    actually stale). The redirect-based approach is reliable because Maimai's
    92→    server-side auth check only fires on real page navigations, and it will
    93→    redirect to the login page if the session is invalid.
    94→
    95→    Args:
    96→        page: Playwright page instance
    97→
    98→    Returns:
    99→        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
   100→    """
   101→    try:
   102→        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
   103→        # The search center page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   104→        protected_url = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
   105→        await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   106→
   107→        # Wait for any redirects to settle (server-side auth redirect happens
   108→        # after initial page load and JS execution)
   109→        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   110→
   111→        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   112→        final_url = page.url
   113→        if 'login' in final_url.lower():
   114→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   115→            return False
   116→
   117→        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   118→        # even if URL didn't change (some login overlays don't change the URL)
   119→        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   120→            () => {
   121→                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   122→                const loginIndicators = [
   123→                    '欢迎使用脉脉',
   124→                    '登录/注册',
   125→                    '请输入手机号',
   126→                    '获取验证码',
   127→                    '扫码登录',
   128→                    '手机号登录'
   129→                ];
   130→                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   131→                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   132→                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   133→                    }
   134→                }
   135→
   136→                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   137→                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   138→                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="手机"], ' +
   139→                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"]'
   140→                );
   141→                if (loginForm) {
   142→                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   143→                }
   144→
   145→                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   146→            }
   147→        """)
   148→
   149→        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   150→            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   151→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   152→            return False
   153→
   154→        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   155→        return True
   156→
   157→    except Exception as e:
   158→        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   159→        return False
   160→
   161→
   162→async def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   163→    """
   164→    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   165→
   166→    Args:
   167→        browser: Browser manager instance
   168→
   169→    Returns:
   170→        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   171→
   172→    Raises:
   173→        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   174→    """
   175→    if browser is None:
   176→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   177→
   178→    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
   179→    if not browser.is_running:
   180→        await browser.start()
   181→
   182→    # Check login status
   183→    if browser.page:
   184→        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   185→            return True
   186→
   187→    # Not logged in — guide user
   188→    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")
   189→
   190→    if await login(browser):
   191→        return True
   192→
   193→    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to Maimai. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")
   194→

1→"""Profile extraction module for Maimai.
     2→
     3→Extracts detailed candidate information from profile pages
     4→including education, work experience, and skills.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict, field
     8→from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any
     9→
    10→from patchright.async_api import Page
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→from rich.panel import Panel
    14→
    15→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    16→from src.core.anti_detection import human_scroll
    17→from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    18→from src.utils.config import get_config
    19→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    20→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    21→from src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    22→from src.utils.validators import validate_maimai_id
    23→from src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    24→from src.exceptions.errors import ExtractionError
    25→
    26→console = Console()
    27→
    28→# Maimai profile URL template
    29→MAIMAI_PROFILE_URL = "https://maimai.cn/contact/detail/{maimai_id}"
    30→
    31→
    32→@dataclass
    33→class Education:
    34→    """Education history entry."""
    35→    school: str
    36→    degree: Optional[str] = None
    37→    major: Optional[str] = None
    38→    start_year: Optional[str] = None
    39→    end_year: Optional[str] = None
    40→
    41→
    42→@dataclass
    43→class WorkExperience:
    44→    """Work experience entry."""
    45→    company: str
    46→    title: str
    47→    start_date: Optional[str] = None
    48→    end_date: Optional[str] = None
    49→    description: Optional[str] = None
    50→
    51→
    52→@dataclass
    53→class CandidateDetail:
    54→    """Detailed candidate profile information."""
    55→    maimai_id: str
    56→    name: str
    57→    title: str
    58→    company: str
    59→    location: Optional[str] = None
    60→    profile_url: Optional[str] = None
    61→    experience_years: Optional[str] = None
    62→    education: List[Education] = field(default_factory=list)
    63→    work_experience: List[WorkExperience] = field(default_factory=list)
    64→    skills: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    65→    basic_info: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    66→
    67→
    68→async def extract_profile(
    69→    maimai_id: str,
    70→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None
    71→) -> CandidateDetail:
    72→    """
    73→    Extract detailed profile for a candidate.
    74→
    75→    Args:
    76→        maimai_id: Maimai user ID
    77→        browser: Browser manager instance
    78→
    79→    Returns:
    80→        CandidateDetail with full profile information
    81→
    82→    Raises:
    83→        RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit exceeded
    84→        ExtractionError: If profile extraction fails
    85→    """
    86→    # Validate input
    87→    validate_maimai_id(maimai_id)
    88→
    89→    if browser is None:
    90→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    91→
    92→    config = get_config()
    93→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    94→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
    95→
    96→    # Ensure logged in
    97→    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    98→
    99→    # Check rate limit (waits through min-interval)
   100→    await async_check_rate_limit('view_profile')
   101→
   102→    console.print(f"[cyan]Extracting profile for ID: {maimai_id}...[/cyan]")
   103→
   104→    try:
   105→        page = browser.page
   106→        profile_url = MAIMAI_PROFILE_URL.format(maimai_id=maimai_id)
   107→
   108→        # Navigate to profile
   109→        await retry_navigation(page, profile_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   110→
   111→        # Wait for page load
   112→        await async_random_delay('page_load')
   113→
   114→        # Simulate human behavior - scroll to load content
   115→        await human_scroll(page, 'down', distance=400)
   116→        await async_random_delay('action')
   117→
   118→        # Extract profile data
   119→        profile = await _extract_profile_data(page, maimai_id, profile_url, config)
   120→
   121→        # Log the action
   122→        rate_limiter.log_action('view_profile', {
   123→            'maimai_id': maimai_id,
   124→            'name': profile.name
   125→        })
   126→
   127→        # Update database
   128→        _update_candidate_in_db(db_manager, profile)
   129→
   130→        # Display summary
   131→        _display_profile_summary(profile)
   132→
   133→        return profile
   134→
   135→    except Exception as e:
   136→        logger.error(f"Error extracting profile: {e}")
   137→        await browser.screenshot(f"profile_error_{maimai_id}")
   138→        raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile for {maimai_id}: {e}")
   139→
   140→
   141→async def _extract_profile_data(
   142→    page: Page,
   143→    maimai_id: str,
   144→    profile_url: str,
   145→    config
   146→) -> CandidateDetail:
   147→    """
   148→    Extract all profile data from page.
   149→
   150→    Args:
   151→        page: Playwright page instance
   152→        maimai_id: Candidate's Maimai ID
   153→        profile_url: Profile URL
   154→        config: Config instance
   155→
   156→    Returns:
   157→        CandidateDetail object
   158→    """
   159→    # Extract basic info
   160→    name = await _extract_text(page, [
   161→        config.get_selector('profile.name'),
   162→        'h1[class*="name"]',
   163→        '.profile-name',
   164→        'h1',
   165→    ])
   166→
   167→    title = await _extract_text(page, [
   168→        config.get_selector('profile.title'),
   169→        '[class*="current-title"]',
   170→        '[class*="position"]',
   171→        '.job-title',
   172→    ])
   173→
   174→    company = await _extract_text(page, [
   175→        config.get_selector('profile.company'),
   176→        '[class*="current-company"]',
   177→        '[class*="company-name"]',
   178→        '.company',
   179→    ])
   180→
   181→    location = await _extract_text(page, [
   182→        config.get_selector('profile.location'),
   183→        '[class*="location"]',
   184→        '[class*="city"]',
   185→    ])
   186→
   187→    experience_years = await _extract_text(page, [
   188→        config.get_selector('profile.experience_years'),
   189→        '[class*="experience"]',
   190→        '[class*="years"]',
   191→    ])
   192→
   193→    # Extract education
   194→    education = await _extract_education(page, config)
   195→
   196→    # Extract work experience
   197→    work_experience = await _extract_work_experience(page, config)
   198→
   199→    # Extract skills
   200→    skills = await _extract_skills(page, config)
   201→
   202→    # Build basic info dict
   203→    basic_info = {
   204→        'experience_years': experience_years,
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    return CandidateDetail(
   208→        maimai_id=maimai_id,
   209→        name=name or "Unknown",
   210→        title=title or "",
   211→        company=company or "",
   212→        location=location,
   213→        profile_url=profile_url,
   214→        experience_years=experience_years,
   215→        education=education,
   216→        work_experience=work_experience,
   217→        skills=skills,
   218→        basic_info=basic_info
   219→    )
   220→
   221→
   222→async def _extract_text(parent, selectors: List[str]) -> Optional[str]:
   223→    """
   224→    Try multiple selectors to extract text content from a parent (Page or Element).
   225→
   226→    Args:
   227→        parent: Playwright Page or ElementHandle to query within
   228→        selectors: List of selectors to try
   229→
   230→    Returns:
   231→        Extracted text or None
   232→    """
   233→    for selector in selectors:
   234→        try:
   235→            elem = await parent.query_selector(selector)
   236→            if elem:
   237→                text = await elem.inner_text()
   238→                text = text.strip()
   239→                if text:
   240→                    return text
   241→        except Exception:
   242→            continue
   243→    return None
   244→
   245→
   246→async def _find_section(page: Page, section_selectors: List[str]) -> Optional[Any]:
   247→    """Find a section element on page using multiple selectors.
   248→
   249→    Args:
   250→        page: Playwright page instance
   251→        section_selectors: List of CSS selectors to try
   252→
   253→    Returns:
   254→        Found element or None
   255→    """
   256→    for selector in section_selectors:
   257→        try:
   258→            section = await page.query_selector(selector)
   259→            if section:
   260→                return section
   261→        except Exception:
   262→            continue
   263→    return None
   264→
   265→
   266→async def _find_items(section, item_selectors: List[str]) -> list:
   267→    """Find items within a section using multiple selectors.
   268→
   269→    Args:
   270→        section: Parent element to query within
   271→        item_selectors: List of CSS selectors to try
   272→
   273→    Returns:
   274→        List of found elements (may be empty)
   275→    """
   276→    for selector in item_selectors:
   277→        items = await section.query_selector_all(selector)
   278→        if items:
   279→            return items
   280→    return []
   281→
   282→
   283→async def _extract_education(page: Page, config) -> List[Education]:
   284→    """Extract education history from profile."""
   285→    education_list = []
   286→
   287→    try:
   288→        section = await _find_section(page, [
   289→            config.get_selector('profile.education_section'),
   290→            '[class*="education"]',
   291→            '[class*="edu-section"]',
   292→            '#education',
   293→        ])
   294→        if not section:
   295→            logger.debug("Education section not found")
   296→            return education_list
   297→
   298→        items = await _find_items(section, [
   299→            config.get_selector('profile.education_item'),
   300→            '[class*="edu-item"]',
   301→            '[class*="education-item"]',
   302→            '.timeline-item',
   303→        ])
   304→
   305→        for item in items:
   306→            try:
   307→                school = await _extract_text(item, [
   308→                    '[class*="school"]', '[class*="name"]', 'h4', '.title'
   309→                ])
   310→                degree = await _extract_text(item, [
   311→                    '[class*="degree"]', '[class*="level"]'
   312→                ])
   313→                major = await _extract_text(item, [
   314→                    '[class*="major"]', '[class*="field"]'
   315→                ])
   316→                date_text = await _extract_text(item, [
   317→                    '[class*="date"]', '[class*="time"]', '.period'
   318→                ])
   319→
   320→                start_year, end_year = _parse_date_range(date_text)
   321→
   322→                if school:
   323→                    education_list.append(Education(
   324→                        school=school,
   325→                        degree=degree,
   326→                        major=major,
   327→                        start_year=start_year,
   328→                        end_year=end_year
   329→                    ))
   330→            except Exception as e:
   331→                logger.debug(f"Error extracting education item: {e}")
   332→                continue
   333→
   334→    except Exception as e:
   335→        logger.debug(f"Error extracting education section: {e}")
   336→
   337→    return education_list
   338→
   339→
   340→async def _extract_work_experience(page: Page, config) -> List[WorkExperience]:
   341→    """Extract work experience from profile."""
   342→    work_list = []
   343→
   344→    try:
   345→        section = await _find_section(page, [
   346→            config.get_selector('profile.work_experience_section'),
   347→            '[class*="work-exp"]',
   348→            '[class*="experience"]',
   349→            '[class*="career"]',
   350→            '#experience',
   351→        ])
   352→        if not section:
   353→            logger.debug("Work experience section not found")
   354→            return work_list
   355→
   356→        items = await _find_items(section, [
   357→            config.get_selector('profile.work_experience_item'),
   358→            '[class*="exp-item"]',
   359→            '[class*="work-item"]',
   360→            '.timeline-item',
   361→        ])
   362→
   363→        for item in items:
   364→            try:
   365→                company = await _extract_text(item, [
   366→                    '[class*="company"]', '[class*="org"]', '.company-name'
   367→                ])
   368→                title = await _extract_text(item, [
   369→                    '[class*="title"]', '[class*="position"]', '.job-title'
   370→                ])
   371→                date_text = await _extract_text(item, [
   372→                    '[class*="date"]', '[class*="time"]', '.period'
   373→                ])
   374→                description = await _extract_text(item, [
   375→                    '[class*="desc"]', '[class*="description"]', '.content'
   376→                ])
   377→
   378→                start_date, end_date = _parse_date_range(date_text)
   379→
   380→                if company and title:
   381→                    work_list.append(WorkExperience(
   382→                        company=company,
   383→                        title=title,
   384→                        start_date=start_date,
   385→                        end_date=end_date,
   386→                        description=description
   387→                    ))
   388→            except Exception as e:
   389→                logger.debug(f"Error extracting work item: {e}")
   390→                continue
   391→
   392→    except Exception as e:
   393→        logger.debug(f"Error extracting work experience section: {e}")
   394→
   395→    return work_list
   396→
   397→
   398→async def _extract_skills(page: Page, config) -> List[str]:
   399→    """Extract skills from profile."""
   400→    skills = []
   401→
   402→    try:
   403→        section = await _find_section(page, [
   404→            config.get_selector('profile.skills_section'),
   405→            '[class*="skills"]',
   406→            '[class*="skill-section"]',
   407→            '#skills',
   408→        ])
   409→        if not section:
   410→            # Try to find skill tags directly on page
   411→            section = page
   412→
   413→        tags = await _find_items(section, [
   414→            config.get_selector('profile.skill_tag'),
   415→            '[class*="skill-tag"]',
   416→            '[class*="skill-item"]',
   417→            '.tag',
   418→        ])
   419→
   420→        for tag in tags:
   421→            try:
   422→                text = await tag.inner_text()
   423→                text = text.strip()
   424→                if text and text not in skills:
   425→                    skills.append(text)
   426→            except Exception:
   427→                continue
   428→
   429→    except Exception as e:
   430→        logger.debug(f"Error extracting skills: {e}")
   431→
   432→    return skills
   433→
   434→
   435→
   436→
   437→def _parse_date_range(date_text: Optional[str]) -> tuple:
   438→    """
   439→    Parse date range string into start and end dates.
   440→
   441→    Args:
   442→        date_text: Date text like "2020-2023" or "2020.01 - 至今"
   443→
   444→    Returns:
   445→        Tuple of (start_date, end_date)
   446→    """
   447→    if not date_text:
   448→        return None, None
   449→
   450→    import re
   451→
   452→    # Common patterns
   453→    patterns = [
   454→        r'(\d{4})[.\-/年]?\s*[-–至到]\s*(\d{4}|至今|现在|present)',
   455→        r'(\d{4})[.\-/](\d{1,2})?\s*[-–至到]\s*(\d{4}|至今|现在|present)',
   456→    ]
   457→
   458→    for pattern in patterns:
   459→        match = re.search(pattern, date_text, re.IGNORECASE)
   460→        if match:
   461→            groups = match.groups()
   462→            start = groups[0]
   463→            end = groups[-1]
   464→            if end in ['至今', '现在', 'present']:
   465→                end = 'present'
   466→            return start, end
   467→
   468→    return None, None
   469→
   470→
   471→def _update_candidate_in_db(db_manager, profile: CandidateDetail) -> None:
   472→    """Update candidate record in database with full profile."""
   473→    try:
   474→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   475→            candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(
   476→                maimai_id=profile.maimai_id
   477→            ).first()
   478→
   479→            if candidate:
   480→                candidate.name = profile.name
   481→                candidate.title = profile.title
   482→                candidate.company = profile.company
   483→                candidate.location = profile.location
   484→                candidate.profile_url = profile.profile_url
   485→                candidate.basic_info = profile.basic_info
   486→                candidate.education = [asdict(e) for e in profile.education]
   487→                candidate.work_experience = [asdict(w) for w in profile.work_experience]
   488→                candidate.skills = profile.skills
   489→            else:
   490→                candidate = Candidate(
   491→                    maimai_id=profile.maimai_id,
   492→                    name=profile.name,
   493→                    title=profile.title,
   494→                    company=profile.company,
   495→                    location=profile.location,
   496→                    profile_url=profile.profile_url,
   497→                    basic_info=profile.basic_info,
   498→                    education=[asdict(e) for e in profile.education],
   499→                    work_experience=[asdict(w) for w in profile.work_experience],
   500→                    skills=profile.skills
   501→                )
   502→                session.add(candidate)
   503→
   504→            logger.debug(f"Updated candidate {profile.maimai_id} in database")
   505→
   506→    except Exception as e:
   507→        logger.error(f"Failed to update candidate in database: {e}")
   508→
   509→
   510→def _display_profile_summary(profile: CandidateDetail) -> None:
   511→    """Display profile summary in terminal."""
   512→    edu_str = ""
   513→    if profile.education:
   514→        edu = profile.education[0]
   515→        edu_str = f"\n  Education: {edu.school}"
   516→        if edu.degree:
   517→            edu_str += f" ({edu.degree})"
   518→
   519→    work_str = ""
   520→    if profile.work_experience:
   521→        work = profile.work_experience[0]
   522→        work_str = f"\n  Experience: {work.title} @ {work.company}"
   523→
   524→    skills_str = ""
   525→    if profile.skills:
   526→        skills_str = f"\n  Skills: {', '.join(profile.skills[:5])}"
   527→        if len(profile.skills) > 5:
   528→            skills_str += f" (+{len(profile.skills) - 5} more)"
   529→
   530→    console.print(Panel(
   531→        f"[bold]{profile.name}[/bold]\n"
   532→        f"  {profile.title} @ {profile.company}"
   533→        f"{edu_str}{work_str}{skills_str}",
   534→        title=f"Profile: {profile.maimai_id}",
   535→        border_style="green"
   536→    ))
   537→

1→"""Export module for exporting candidate data.
     2→
     3→Supports Excel (.xlsx) and CSV formats with optional
     4→message history inclusion.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from datetime import datetime
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Optional, Literal, List, Dict, Any
    10→
    11→import pandas as pd
    12→from openpyxl import Workbook
    13→from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
    14→from openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows
    15→from loguru import logger
    16→from rich.console import Console
    17→
    18→from src.utils.config import get_config
    19→from src.models.database import Candidate, Message, get_db_manager
    20→
    21→console = Console()
    22→
    23→
    24→def export_candidates(
    25→    format: Literal["excel", "xlsx", "csv"] = "excel",
    26→    output_path: Optional[str] = None,
    27→    include_messages: bool = True
    28→) -> str:
    29→    """
    30→    Export candidate data to Excel or CSV.
    31→
    32→    Args:
    33→        format: Export format ('excel', 'xlsx', or 'csv')
    34→        output_path: Output file path (auto-generated if None)
    35→        include_messages: Include message history
    36→
    37→    Returns:
    38→        Path to exported file
    39→    """
    40→    config = get_config()
    41→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
    42→
    43→    # Normalize format
    44→    if format == "xlsx":
    45→        format = "excel"
    46→
    47→    # Generate output path if not provided
    48→    if output_path is None:
    49→        export_dir = Path(config.get('data.export_dir', 'data/exports'))
    50→        export_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    51→
    52→        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
    53→        extension = 'xlsx' if format == 'excel' else 'csv'
    54→        output_path = str(export_dir / f"candidates_{timestamp}.{extension}")
    55→
    56→    # Ensure parent directory exists
    57→    Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    58→
    59→    # Get data from database
    60→    candidates_data = _get_candidates_data(db_manager, include_messages)
    61→
    62→    if not candidates_data:
    63→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates to export[/yellow]")
    64→        return ""
    65→
    66→    # Create DataFrame
    67→    df = _create_dataframe(candidates_data)
    68→
    69→    # Export based on format
    70→    if format == "excel":
    71→        _export_to_excel(df, output_path)
    72→    else:
    73→        _export_to_csv(df, output_path)
    74→
    75→    console.print(f"[green]Exported {len(candidates_data)} candidates to: {output_path}[/green]")
    76→    return output_path
    77→
    78→
    79→def _get_candidates_data(db_manager, include_messages: bool) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    80→    """
    81→    Get candidate data from database.
    82→
    83→    Args:
    84→        db_manager: Database manager instance
    85→        include_messages: Whether to include message history
    86→
    87→    Returns:
    88→        List of candidate dictionaries
    89→    """
    90→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
    91→        candidates = session.query(Candidate).all()
    92→
    93→        data = []
    94→        for candidate in candidates:
    95→            record = {
    96→                'maimai_id': candidate.maimai_id,
    97→                'name': candidate.name,
    98→                'title': candidate.title,
    99→                'company': candidate.company,
   100→                'location': candidate.location,
   101→                'profile_url': candidate.profile_url,
   102→                'first_seen': candidate.first_seen,
   103→                'last_updated': candidate.last_updated,
   104→                'source_search': candidate.source_search,
   105→            }
   106→
   107→            # Flatten education
   108→            education = candidate.education or []
   109→            if education:
   110→                latest_edu = education[0]
   111→                record['latest_education'] = _format_education(latest_edu)
   112→            else:
   113→                record['latest_education'] = ''
   114→
   115→            # Flatten work experience
   116→            work_exp = candidate.work_experience or []
   117→            if work_exp:
   118→                latest_work = work_exp[0]
   119→                record['latest_experience'] = _format_work_experience(latest_work)
   120→            else:
   121→                record['latest_experience'] = ''
   122→
   123→            # Format skills
   124→            skills = candidate.skills or []
   125→            record['skills'] = ', '.join(skills) if skills else ''
   126→
   127→            # Get message info if requested
   128→            if include_messages:
   129→                messages = session.query(Message).filter_by(
   130→                    candidate_maimai_id=candidate.maimai_id
   131→                ).order_by(Message.created_at.desc()).all()
   132→
   133→                record['message_count'] = len(messages)
   134→
   135→                if messages:
   136→                    latest_msg = messages[0]
   137→                    record['last_message_status'] = latest_msg.status
   138→                    record['last_message_date'] = latest_msg.sent_at or latest_msg.created_at
   139→                    record['last_message_template'] = latest_msg.template_name
   140→                else:
   141→                    record['last_message_status'] = ''
   142→                    record['last_message_date'] = None
   143→                    record['last_message_template'] = ''
   144→
   145→            data.append(record)
   146→
   147→        return data
   148→
   149→
   150→def _format_education(edu: Dict) -> str:
   151→    """Format education entry as string."""
   152→    parts = []
   153→    if edu.get('school'):
   154→        parts.append(edu['school'])
   155→    if edu.get('degree'):
   156→        parts.append(edu['degree'])
   157→    if edu.get('major'):
   158→        parts.append(edu['major'])
   159→    return ' - '.join(parts) if parts else ''
   160→
   161→
   162→def _format_work_experience(work: Dict) -> str:
   163→    """Format work experience entry as string."""
   164→    parts = []
   165→    if work.get('title'):
   166→        parts.append(work['title'])
   167→    if work.get('company'):
   168→        parts.append(f"@ {work['company']}")
   169→    return ' '.join(parts) if parts else ''
   170→
   171→
   172→def _create_dataframe(data: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> pd.DataFrame:
   173→    """
   174→    Create pandas DataFrame from candidate data.
   175→
   176→    Args:
   177→        data: List of candidate dictionaries
   178→
   179→    Returns:
   180→        pandas DataFrame
   181→    """
   182→    # Define column order
   183→    columns = [
   184→        'maimai_id',
   185→        'name',
   186→        'title',
   187→        'company',
   188→        'location',
   189→        'latest_education',
   190→        'latest_experience',
   191→        'skills',
   192→        'profile_url',
   193→        'source_search',
   194→        'first_seen',
   195→        'last_updated',
   196→    ]
   197→
   198→    # Add message columns if present
   199→    if data and 'message_count' in data[0]:
   200→        columns.extend([
   201→            'message_count',
   202→            'last_message_status',
   203→            'last_message_date',
   204→            'last_message_template',
   205→        ])
   206→
   207→    # Create DataFrame
   208→    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
   209→
   210→    # Reorder columns (only include existing columns)
   211→    existing_columns = [c for c in columns if c in df.columns]
   212→    df = df[existing_columns]
   213→
   214→    # Format datetime columns
   215→    datetime_columns = ['first_seen', 'last_updated', 'last_message_date']
   216→    for col in datetime_columns:
   217→        if col in df.columns:
   218→            df[col] = pd.to_datetime(df[col]).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')
   219→
   220→    return df
   221→
   222→
   223→def _export_to_excel(df: pd.DataFrame, output_path: str) -> None:
   224→    """
   225→    Export DataFrame to Excel with formatting.
   226→
   227→    Args:
   228→        df: pandas DataFrame
   229→        output_path: Output file path
   230→    """
   231→    # Create workbook
   232→    wb = Workbook()
   233→    ws = wb.active
   234→    ws.title = "Candidates"
   235→
   236→    # Define styles
   237→    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   238→    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   239→    header_alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   240→
   241→    border = Border(
   242→        left=Side(style='thin'),
   243→        right=Side(style='thin'),
   244→        top=Side(style='thin'),
   245→        bottom=Side(style='thin')
   246→    )
   247→
   248→    # Write data
   249→    for r_idx, row in enumerate(dataframe_to_rows(df, index=False, header=True), 1):
   250→        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row, 1):
   251→            cell = ws.cell(row=r_idx, column=c_idx, value=value)
   252→            cell.border = border
   253→
   254→            if r_idx == 1:
   255→                # Header row
   256→                cell.font = header_font
   257→                cell.fill = header_fill
   258→                cell.alignment = header_alignment
   259→            else:
   260→                # Data rows
   261→                cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   262→
   263→    # Set column widths
   264→    column_widths = {
   265→        'A': 15,  # maimai_id
   266→        'B': 12,  # name
   267→        'C': 25,  # title
   268→        'D': 20,  # company
   269→        'E': 12,  # location
   270→        'F': 30,  # latest_education
   271→        'G': 30,  # latest_experience
   272→        'H': 40,  # skills
   273→        'I': 40,  # profile_url
   274→        'J': 20,  # source_search
   275→        'K': 18,  # first_seen
   276→        'L': 18,  # last_updated
   277→        'M': 12,  # message_count
   278→        'N': 15,  # last_message_status
   279→        'O': 18,  # last_message_date
   280→        'P': 15,  # last_message_template
   281→    }
   282→
   283→    for col, width in column_widths.items():
   284→        ws.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   285→
   286→    # Freeze first row
   287→    ws.freeze_panes = 'A2'
   288→
   289→    # Add auto-filter
   290→    ws.auto_filter.ref = ws.dimensions
   291→
   292→    # Save workbook
   293→    wb.save(output_path)
   294→    logger.info(f"Excel file saved to: {output_path}")
   295→
   296→
   297→def _export_to_csv(df: pd.DataFrame, output_path: str) -> None:
   298→    """
   299→    Export DataFrame to CSV.
   300→
   301→    Args:
   302→        df: pandas DataFrame
   303→        output_path: Output file path
   304→    """
   305→    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False, encoding='utf-8-sig')
   306→    logger.info(f"CSV file saved to: {output_path}")
   307→

1→"""Interactive selector discovery for chat pages.
     2→
     3→When automatic selectors fail, this module pauses execution and lets
     4→the user manually navigate to the correct chat page in the browser.
     5→It then inspects the DOM to discover working selectors, which are
     6→reused for all remaining candidates in the session.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import asyncio
    10→from dataclasses import dataclass
    11→from typing import Optional
    12→
    13→from patchright.async_api import Page
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→from rich.console import Console
    16→from rich.panel import Panel
    17→from rich.text import Text
    18→
    19→from src.core.anti_detection import human_type, human_click
    20→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    21→
    22→console = Console()
    23→
    24→# JavaScript to scan DOM for interactive elements
    25→DISCOVER_ELEMENTS_JS = """
    26→() => {
    27→    const results = [];
    28→
    29→    // Find visible buttons
    30→    const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button, [role="button"], a[class*="btn"], div[class*="btn"]');
    31→    buttons.forEach((el, i) => {
    32→        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    33→        if (rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0) {
    34→            const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().substring(0, 50);
    35→            const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
    36→            const classes = el.className ? String(el.className).substring(0, 80) : '';
    37→            const id = el.id || '';
    38→            results.push({
    39→                type: 'button',
    40→                index: results.length,
    41→                tag: tag,
    42→                text: text,
    43→                classes: classes,
    44→                id: id,
    45→                selector: id ? `#${id}` : (classes ? `${tag}.${String(el.className).split(' ')[0]}` : `${tag}:nth-of-type(${i+1})`),
    46→            });
    47→        }
    48→    });
    49→
    50→    // Find visible textareas and contenteditable elements
    51→    const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('textarea, [contenteditable="true"], input[type="text"]');
    52→    inputs.forEach((el, i) => {
    53→        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    54→        if (rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0) {
    55→            const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
    56→            const classes = el.className ? String(el.className).substring(0, 80) : '';
    57→            const id = el.id || '';
    58→            const placeholder = el.getAttribute('placeholder') || '';
    59→            const contenteditable = el.getAttribute('contenteditable') || '';
    60→            results.push({
    61→                type: 'input',
    62→                index: results.length,
    63→                tag: tag,
    64→                text: placeholder,
    65→                classes: classes,
    66→                id: id,
    67→                contenteditable: contenteditable,
    68→                selector: id ? `#${id}` : (classes ? `${tag}.${String(el.className).split(' ')[0]}` : (contenteditable ? '[contenteditable="true"]' : `${tag}:nth-of-type(${i+1})`)),
    69→            });
    70→        }
    71→    });
    72→
    73→    return results;
    74→}
    75→"""
    76→
    77→MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES = 3
    78→
    79→
    80→@dataclass
    81→class DiscoveredSelectors:
    82→    """Session-scoped container for discovered chat page selectors."""
    83→
    84→    chat_button: Optional[str] = None
    85→    message_input: Optional[str] = None
    86→    send_button: Optional[str] = None
    87→    chat_url_pattern: Optional[str] = None
    88→    skip_chat_button: bool = False
    89→    is_populated: bool = False
    90→
    91→
    92→async def discover_chat_page(
    93→    page: Page,
    94→    maimai_id: str,
    95→    browser,
    96→) -> DiscoveredSelectors:
    97→    """
    98→    Interactive flow to discover chat page selectors.
    99→
   100→    Pauses execution and instructs the user to manually navigate to the
   101→    chat page in the browser. Then inspects the DOM and lets the user
   102→    select which elements are the message input and send button.
   103→
   104→    Args:
   105→        page: Current Playwright page
   106→        maimai_id: Current candidate's Maimai ID (used to derive URL pattern)
   107→        browser: BrowserManager instance
   108→
   109→    Returns:
   110→        Populated DiscoveredSelectors instance
   111→    """
   112→    discovered = DiscoveredSelectors()
   113→    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   114→
   115→    # Step 1: Instruct user to navigate to chat page
   116→    instructions = Text()
   117→    instructions.append("Automatic chat selectors not found.\n\n", style="bold red")
   118→    instructions.append("Please do the following in the browser window:\n", style="bold")
   119→    instructions.append("1. Navigate to the chat/message page for this candidate\n")
   120→    instructions.append("2. Make sure the message input is visible on screen\n")
   121→    instructions.append("3. Come back here and press Enter when ready\n\n")
   122→    instructions.append(f"Candidate ID: ", style="dim")
   123→    instructions.append(f"{maimai_id}", style="cyan")
   124→
   125→    console.print(Panel(
   126→        instructions,
   127→        title="Interactive Selector Discovery",
   128→        border_style="yellow",
   129→        padding=(1, 2),
   130→    ))
   131→
   132→    for attempt in range(1, MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES + 1):
   133→        await loop.run_in_executor(None, lambda: input("\nPress Enter when the chat page is ready..."))
   134→
   135→        # Step 2: Capture current URL and derive pattern
   136→        current_url = page.url
   137→        logger.debug(f"Current URL after user navigation: {current_url}")
   138→
   139→        if maimai_id in current_url:
   140→            discovered.chat_url_pattern = current_url.replace(maimai_id, "{maimai_id}")
   141→            console.print(f"[green]URL pattern detected:[/green] {discovered.chat_url_pattern}")
   142→        else:
   143→            console.print("[yellow]Could not derive URL pattern (maimai_id not found in URL).[/yellow]")
   144→            console.print(f"[dim]Current URL: {current_url}[/dim]")
   145→
   146→        # Step 3: Scan DOM for elements
   147→        elements = await page.evaluate(DISCOVER_ELEMENTS_JS)
   148→
   149→        if not elements:
   150→            console.print(f"[yellow]No interactive elements found (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES}).[/yellow]")
   151→            if attempt < MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES:
   152→                console.print("[dim]Make sure the chat page is fully loaded and try again.[/dim]")
   153→                continue
   154→            else:
   155→                console.print("[red]Giving up after max retries. Selector discovery failed.[/red]")
   156→                return discovered
   157→
   158→        # Step 4: Display discovered elements
   159→        input_elements = [e for e in elements if e['type'] == 'input']
   160→        button_elements = [e for e in elements if e['type'] == 'button']
   161→
   162→        # Select message input
   163→        if input_elements:
   164→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Input elements found:[/bold cyan]")
   165→            for i, el in enumerate(input_elements):
   166→                label = el.get('text') or el.get('classes', '') or el['tag']
   167→                console.print(f"  [{i}] {el['tag']} - {label} (selector: {el['selector']})")
   168→
   169→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   170→                None,
   171→                lambda: input(f"\nSelect message input [0-{len(input_elements)-1}] (default 0): ").strip()
   172→            )
   173→            idx = int(choice) if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(input_elements) else 0
   174→            discovered.message_input = input_elements[idx]['selector']
   175→            console.print(f"[green]Message input:[/green] {discovered.message_input}")
   176→        else:
   177→            console.print("[yellow]No input elements found.[/yellow]")
   178→            if attempt < MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES:
   179→                continue
   180→            return discovered
   181→
   182→        # Select send button
   183→        if button_elements:
   184→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Button elements found:[/bold cyan]")
   185→            for i, el in enumerate(button_elements):
   186→                label = el.get('text') or el.get('classes', '') or el['tag']
   187→                console.print(f"  [{i}] {el['tag']} - \"{label}\" (selector: {el['selector']})")
   188→
   189→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   190→                None,
   191→                lambda: input(f"\nSelect send button [0-{len(button_elements)-1}]: ").strip()
   192→            )
   193→            if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(button_elements):
   194→                discovered.send_button = button_elements[int(choice)]['selector']
   195→                console.print(f"[green]Send button:[/green] {discovered.send_button}")
   196→            else:
   197→                console.print("[yellow]No send button selected.[/yellow]")
   198→
   199→        # Step 5: Ask about chat button
   200→        needs_chat = await loop.run_in_executor(
   201→            None,
   202→            lambda: input("\nDoes reaching the chat page require clicking a button first? [y/N]: ").strip().lower()
   203→        )
   204→
   205→        if needs_chat == 'y':
   206→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Select the chat button from the button list above:[/bold cyan]")
   207→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   208→                None,
   209→                lambda: input(f"Chat button index [0-{len(button_elements)-1}]: ").strip()
   210→            )
   211→            if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(button_elements):
   212→                discovered.chat_button = button_elements[int(choice)]['selector']
   213→                console.print(f"[green]Chat button:[/green] {discovered.chat_button}")
   214→        else:
   215→            discovered.skip_chat_button = True
   216→
   217→        # Mark as populated if we have at least message_input
   218→        if discovered.message_input:
   219→            discovered.is_populated = True
   220→            console.print(Panel(
   221→                "[green]Selector discovery complete! These selectors will be reused for remaining candidates.[/green]",
   222→                border_style="green",
   223→            ))
   224→            break
   225→
   226→    return discovered
   227→
   228→
   229→async def apply_discovered_selectors(
   230→    page: Page,
   231→    maimai_id: str,
   232→    discovered: DiscoveredSelectors,
   233→    content: str,
   234→) -> bool:
   235→    """
   236→    Send a message using previously discovered selectors.
   237→
   238→    Args:
   239→        page: Playwright page instance
   240→        maimai_id: Candidate's Maimai ID
   241→        discovered: Previously discovered selectors
   242→        content: Message content to send
   243→
   244→    Returns:
   245→        True if message sent successfully
   246→    """
   247→    try:
   248→        # Navigate to chat URL if pattern is available
   249→        if discovered.chat_url_pattern:
   250→            chat_url = discovered.chat_url_pattern.format(maimai_id=maimai_id)
   251→            await page.goto(chat_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   252→            await async_random_delay('page_load')
   253→
   254→        # Click chat button if needed
   255→        if not discovered.skip_chat_button and discovered.chat_button:
   256→            try:
   257→                await human_click(page, discovered.chat_button)
   258→                await async_random_delay('action')
   259→            except Exception as e:
   260→                logger.warning(f"Failed to click chat button: {e}")
   261→                return False
   262→
   263→        # Type message
   264→        if discovered.message_input:
   265→            try:
   266→                await page.wait_for_selector(discovered.message_input, timeout=10000)
   267→                await human_type(page, discovered.message_input, content)
   268→                await async_random_delay('action')
   269→            except Exception as e:
   270→                logger.warning(f"Failed to type message: {e}")
   271→                return False
   272→
   273→        # Click send
   274→        if discovered.send_button:
   275→            try:
   276→                await human_click(page, discovered.send_button)
   277→                await async_random_delay('action')
   278→
   279→                # Verify message was sent
   280→                from src.modules.message import _verify_message_sent
   281→                verified = await _verify_message_sent(page, content)
   282→                if not verified:
   283→                    logger.warning("Message send via discovered selectors could not be verified")
   284→                else:
   285→                    logger.debug("Message send via discovered selectors verified")
   286→                return True
   287→            except Exception as e:
   288→                logger.warning(f"Failed to click send: {e}")
   289→                return False
   290→
   291→        logger.warning("No send button selector available")
   292→        return False
   293→
   294→    except Exception as e:
   295→        logger.error(f"Error applying discovered selectors: {e}")
   296→        return False
   297→

    53→def _is_non_rd_title(title: str) -> bool:
    54→    """Check if a title indicates a non-R&D role.
    55→
    56→    Strict mode: if any non-R&D keyword appears, filter out regardless of
    57→    whether R&D keywords are also present (e.g. "技术产品经理" is filtered).
    58→    Returns False (keep) only if title is empty or contains no non-R&D keywords.
    59→    """
    60→    if not title:
    61→        return False
    62→
    63→    # Strict: any non-R&D keyword present → filter out
    64→    if any(kw in title for kw in NON_RD_KEYWORDS):
    65→        return True
    66→
    67→    return False
